summary: American surrealist filmmaker and multimedia artist (1946–2025). Known for dreamlike, disturbing imagery and nonlinear narratives. Major works include Blue Velvet, Twin Peaks (with Mark Frost), Mulholland Drive, The Elephant Man, and Eraserhead. Longtime collaborator with composer Angelo Badalamenti. Also a painter, musician, and writer (book: Catching the Big Fish).

works.name: Blue Velvet (1986)
works.description: Opens with an extreme close-up of beetles warring in the grass and the discovery of a severed ear in a field. Jeffrey Beaumont watches from a closet as nightclub singer Dorothy Vallens (played by Isabella Rossellini) is ritualistically raped by the gas-huffing, PBR-obsessed Frank Booth (Dennis Hopper). Frank exclaims 'Heineken? Fuck that shit! Pabst! Blue! Ribbon!' Isabella Rossellini performs the title song as Dorothy Vallens. Jeffrey shoots the Yellow Man in the film. A David Foster Wallace essay titled '[Lynch] Keeps His Head' discusses a scene in which a woman covers her face in red lipstick. Wikipedia

works.name: Twin Peaks (TV series, 1990–1991; 2017)
works.description: A TV series co-created with Mark Frost, themed around the murder of Laura Palmer. The series theme 'Falling' is sung by Julee Cruise (Lynch wrote the lyrics). The score is by Angelo Badalamenti. Agent Dale Cooper famously praises 'a damn fine cup of coffee.' A depiction of the Trinity test is accompanied by Penderecki's Threnody for the Victims of Hiroshima in Twin Peaks: The Return (2017). Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me (the film prequel) shows Cherubini's C minor Requiem as Leland kills his daughter in a train car, wrapping her in plastic. Dougie Jones is a 'tulpa' of Agent Cooper who becomes trapped in the Black Lodge; Kyle MacLachlan plays both. Alice in Wonderland connections are referenced in the first episode (a 'cottontail rabbit'). Wikipedia

works.name: Mulholland Drive (2001)
works.description: A surrealist mystery set in Hollywood. Diane Selwyn resembles/is aspiring actress Betty Elms; the two characters (played by Naomi Watts and Laura Harring) shift identities when a mysterious blue box is opened. A creepy monster emerges from behind a diner. A woman believing herself to be Rita Hayworth discovers the body of Diane Selwyn. Lynch paid tribute to the doubling theme of Bergman's Persona. Wikipedia

works.name: The Elephant Man (1980)
works.description: A 1980 film. John Hurt spent eight hours a day attaching prosthetics to portray Joseph Merrick. Barber's Adagio for Strings plays as the John Hurt character dies in his sleep, after which Tennyson's 'Nothing Will Die' is recited (this predated Oliver Stone's Platoon's use of the same piece). Wikipedia

works.name: Eraserhead (1977)
works.description: Lynch's debut feature. Opens with a giant floating sperm emerging from the main character's mouth. Follows Henry Spencer and Mary X as they try to care for and ultimately kill a horrifying skinless baby. Wikipedia

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: American filmmaker and multimedia artist David Lynch (1946–2025). Longtime music collaborator: Angelo Badalamenti (first met through Blue Velvet). Lynch wrote the book Catching the Big Fish about creativity and Transcendental Meditation. He also played slide guitar on BlueBOB (with John Neff) and made a painting called Rock with Seven Eyes. A 2019 Bonnefanten Museum retrospective displayed his surrealist artworks. He filmed a 13-minute shot of a cheese head in front of power lines for a film at the Festival of Disruption. David Foster Wallace wrote an essay titled '[Lynch] Keeps His Head.' Lynch ordered a chocolate milkshake at Bob's Big Boy every day at 2:30 pm for seven years. He and Mark Frost co-created Twin Peaks. Kyle MacLachlan frequently starred in his work. Wikipedia

comprehensive_summary:
David Lynch (1946–2025) was an American surrealist filmmaker and multimedia artist known for dreamlike, disturbing imagery and nonlinear narratives. He also painted (a 2019 Bonnefanten Museum retrospective displayed surrealist works like Rock with Seven Eyes and Boy Lights Fire), played slide guitar on an album (BlueBOB with John Neff), and wrote the book Catching the Big Fish about creativity and Transcendental Meditation. David Foster Wallace wrote an essay titled '[Lynch] Keeps His Head' about him. Lynch ordered a chocolate milkshake at Bob's Big Boy every day at 2:30 pm for seven years.

Blue Velvet (1986) opens with an extreme close-up of beetles warring in the grass and the discovery of a severed ear in a field. Dorothy Vallens (Isabella Rossellini) is a nightclub singer ritualistically raped by gas-huffing Frank Booth (Dennis Hopper) while Jeffrey Beaumont watches from a closet. Frank exclaims 'Heineken? Fuck that shit! Pabst! Blue! Ribbon!' — a line inspired by a fast food customer Lynch observed. Angelo Badalamenti was originally hired as Rossellini's singing coach for this film, beginning a lasting creative partnership with Lynch.

Twin Peaks (1990–91, 2017), co-created with Mark Frost, follows the murder of Laura Palmer. The theme 'Falling' was sung by Julee Cruise (lyrics by Lynch) and scored by Badalamenti. Agent Dale Cooper is known for praising 'a damn fine cup of coffee.' In the prequel film Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me, Cherubini's Requiem in C Minor plays as Leland kills his daughter in a train car and wraps her in plastic. Twin Peaks: The Return (2017) features a depiction of the Trinity nuclear test accompanied by Penderecki's Threnody for the Victims of Hiroshima. Dougie Jones, a 'tulpa' of Agent Cooper trapped in the Black Lodge and played by Kyle MacLachlan, was inspired by Peter Sellers's performance in Being There.

Mulholland Drive (2001) is a surrealist Hollywood mystery starring Naomi Watts and Laura Harring, whose characters shift identities when a mysterious blue box is opened. A creepy monster emerges from behind a diner. A woman believing herself to be Rita Hayworth discovers the body of Diane Selwyn. Lynch paid tribute to the doubling theme of Bergman's Persona.

The Elephant Man (1980) starred John Hurt, who spent eight hours a day in prosthetics to portray Joseph Merrick. Barber's Adagio for Strings plays as his character dies in his sleep, followed by a recitation of Tennyson's 'Nothing Will Die' — this predated Oliver Stone's more famous use of the same piece in Platoon.

Eraserhead (1977), Lynch's debut feature, opens with a giant floating sperm emerging from the protagonist Henry Spencer's mouth; Henry and Mary X struggle to care for and ultimately kill a horrifying skinless baby.
